entries.summary: 23 scenes, avg Q:8.9, 23 standalone -- This video is a highly effective and visually stunning trailer for Mechabellum. It expertly blends strategic gameplay with intense, large-scale combat, showcasing a wide variety of units, customization options, and game modes. The audio design is powerful, with impactful sound effects and a driving hybrid score that perfectly complements the on-screen action. The use of review quotes adds credibility, and the climactic triple nuclear explosion is an unforgettable moment. The pacing is excellent, building from strategic deployment to overwhelming destruction, making it highly engaging and effective for attracting new players.

entries.summary: 15 scenes, avg Q:8.9, 15 standalone -- This video is a high-quality, action-packed trailer effectively showcasing a rich, cosmic fantasy world. It uses a diverse range of visual styles, from grand establishing shots to intense close-up action, and integrates clear text overlays to convey narrative and marketing points. The orchestral hybrid music perfectly complements the visuals, building tension and excitement. The voiceover is clear and compelling, guiding the viewer through the lore and features. The video successfully creates a sense of epic scale, ancient powers, and personal involvement, culminating in a strong call to action for its NFT-based game.

entries.summary: 15 scenes, avg Q:8.7, 15 standalone -- This video effectively blends cinematic storytelling with game and NFT feature showcases. The use of strong visual contrasts (light/dark, fire/ice) and a powerful orchestral score creates an epic and engaging atmosphere. The transitions between different art styles (realistic CGI, anime, game UI) are handled well, maintaining a cohesive narrative. The voiceover adds gravitas and guides the viewer through the lore and features, culminating in a clear call to action. The production value is consistently high, making it a compelling watch.

entries.summary: Complete production audit across 11 trailer versions. Established definitive workflow, banned tools/techniques that failed, codified audio levels, text overlay method, and approval process.
techniques: ffmpeg drawtext for text overlays (not Shotstack HTML)
Post-render text application prevents black bars and allows adjustment
Shotstack HTML overlays create black bars -- banned
Shotstack title presets create black backgrounds -- banned
Screen recordings all failed (broken pages, loading states) -- banned
Ken Burns zoom looks amateur -- banned, use Segmind P2V
Photo overlays (character portraits, logos) look scattered -- banned
Dark overlay tracks dim the whole screen -- banned
Write VO script AFTER assembling clips, not before
Scene-matched VO: voice describes what is on screen
Hard cuts between scenes (no fade transitions)
Clip audio at 0.05 (subtle ambient, not muted)
Timestamp cascade: changing one clip requires recalculating ALL subsequent elements
Never trim clips beyond source duration (causes freeze frames)
Never cut frames from rendered video (breaks audio sync)
Music trim must match video shift exactly
VO starts ON the scene cut, not 0.5s after
Founder approval required before production render

entries.summary: 7 clips for "Watch this entire video carefully. I need a COMPLETE LIST of every visual IMPACT EVENT — explosions, strikes connecting, magic bursts, transformations, ground impacts, energy releases, reveals. For EACH event tell me: 1) The EXACT timestamp to 0.1s precision where the event PEAKS (maximum visual intensity), 2) What the event IS (e.g. 'sword strikes ground with debris explosion'), 3) How BIG the event is on a 1-10 scale (10 = fills entire screen with energy, 1 = subtle flash). I need ALL events, not just the top 5. List every single one.", source Q:8/10

techniques.name: Montage with direct call to action

lessonsLearned: Synchronize strong audio impacts with visual climaxes for maximum emotional effect.
Use top-down views to clearly demonstrate strategic gameplay mechanics like unit deployment and positioning.
Integrate UI elements directly into gameplay footage to provide immediate feedback and highlight game features.
Employ dynamic camera movements (pans, dollies) and extreme wide shots to convey the massive scale of battles and armies.
Utilize text overlays and glitch effects for testimonials and feature highlights to add modern flair and convey information effectively.
Build towards an ultimate, visually spectacular climax (e.g., nuclear explosions) to leave a lasting impression on the viewer.
Effective use of split-screen and contrasting color palettes to represent opposing forces.
Strategic placement of text overlays to convey key information without distracting from visuals.
Synchronization of music swells, beat drops, and SFX with visual climaxes enhances emotional impact.
Blending diverse art styles (realistic CGI, anime, stylized fantasy) can create a unique and expansive world feel.
A strong voiceover can effectively narrate complex lore and guide the viewer through a fast-paced trailer.
Effective use of contrasting visuals and dialogue can create intrigue and depth in lore.
Syncing powerful sound effects (like explosions and roars) with visual climaxes significantly enhances impact.
Clear UI showcases are crucial for communicating game mechanics and economic models in blockchain games.
The use of symbolic, grand imagery for factions and ancient powers builds a rich, epic atmosphere.
Integrating direct calls to action (like 'Mint Now' and website URLs) within visually appealing scenes is highly effective.
Effective use of voiceover to narrate complex lore quickly and add gravitas.
Seamless integration of diverse visual styles (CGI, anime, UI) within a single narrative, maintaining thematic consistency.
Strategic placement of high-energy action sequences and impactful reveals to maintain viewer engagement and excitement.
Clear call-to-action at the end, reinforced by visual and audio cues, to drive user interaction.

failurePatterns.failure: Random clip montage — no visual story coherence
failurePatterns.cause: Clips from 3 different games (FF16, FF7, Genshin) with different art styles, characters, and worlds. Prioritized spike alignment over storytelling.
failurePatterns.fix: Design visual story BEFORE hunting clips. Use ONE visual world. Write a shot list.

failurePatterns.failure: Gemini event timestamps were wrong — dialogue scenes where combat was reported
failurePatterns.cause: Gemini said 'Titan punches' at 143.5s but actual content was dialogue with subtitles. Trusted AI text descriptions without visual verification.
failurePatterns.fix: ALWAYS extract frames at multiple timepoints and visually inspect BEFORE using any clip.

failurePatterns.failure: 4 clips had burned-in subtitles or game UI — found only during QC after render
failurePatterns.cause: Only checked one frame per clip at the expected event moment. Subtitles appeared at other points.
failurePatterns.fix: QC every clip at 4+ timepoints (0.5s, 1.5s, 2.5s, 3.5s). Check the ENTIRE clip, not just the event frame.

failurePatterns.failure: Genshin Impact logo visible in energy-beam clip at 27s
failurePatterns.cause: Extracted from end of Genshin trailer (142.5-146.5s) which includes the game's logo splash.
failurePatterns.fix: Map 'clean zones' for every source. Know where logos/credits appear and avoid those sections.

failurePatterns.failure: No visual faction distinction — both 'gods' and 'demons' scenes looked the same
failurePatterns.cause: All clips were just 'epic moments' without visual contrast. No light-vs-dark palette separation.
failurePatterns.fix: Define visual palettes for each faction BEFORE clip selection. Light/crystal = divine, dark/fire = demonic.

failurePatterns.failure: Static text on flat dark backgrounds — dead air with zero motion
failurePatterns.cause: Used empty dark color backgrounds for text sections instead of animated backgrounds
failurePatterns.fix: Always use animated backgrounds (blockchain nodes, cosmic nebula, neon tunnels) behind text. Never flat dark.

failurePatterns.failure: Loading spinner and Disconnected badge visible in screen recording
failurePatterns.cause: Used raw screen recordings without QC — dev UI state was captured
failurePatterns.fix: Never use raw screen recordings of dev/staging UI. QC at 4+ timepoints before inclusion.

failurePatterns.failure: Mouse cursor and browser URL bar visible in zoomed UI recordings
failurePatterns.cause: Zoomed into screen recordings but cursors and URL bars were still in the crop area
failurePatterns.fix: Abandoned screen recordings entirely. Used YouTube clips from same-genre games instead.

failurePatterns.failure: Reused blockchain-bg clip 3 times — felt lazy and repetitive
failurePatterns.cause: Only had 6 clips for 11 segments, padded with reuse
failurePatterns.fix: Source enough unique clips BEFORE building. 11 segments = 11 unique clips minimum.

failurePatterns.failure: Music too loud (0.45 volume) drowning the VO
failurePatterns.cause: Applied cinematic music levels (0.4) to a promo where VO carries the information
failurePatterns.fix: Crypto promo music at 0.25-0.35. VO must be clearly dominant.

failurePatterns.failure: Low bitrate (660kbps) made all text overlays blurry and unreadable
failurePatterns.cause: Default ffmpeg encoding without specifying bitrate
failurePatterns.fix: Minimum 4000kbps (-b:v 4000k) when text overlays are present.

ff16-shiva-vs-titan.mp4.avoid: 0-26s (human-scale setup), 63-140s (mixed gameplay/dialogue with subtitles), 140+ (heavy subtitles and game UI)
ff16-shiva-vs-titan.mp4.notes: Eikon content is concentrated in first ~65 seconds only

genshin-skirk-trailer.mp4.notes: Best brand-fit (purple/blue cosmic palette matches D&D). Logo appears around 145s.

ff7-advent-children.mp4.notes: Multiple disconnected action scenes. Some have strong fire/destruction visuals.

cinematic-trailer.description: Epic cinematic game trailers using AAA game footage, orchestral music, and dramatic VO
cinematic-trailer.visuals: Game cinematics, AAA clips, single visual world, event-to-spike alignment
cinematic-trailer.text: Sparse overlay via ffmpeg drawtext, only during VO, not during climax
knownFailures: static images pretending to be video

crypto-promo.description: Fast-paced crypto/NFT/web3 hype explainer for Twitter/TikTok scroll-stopping
crypto-promo.music: Electronic/trap beat, high energy from second 1, 140-160 BPM
crypto-promo.voice: Fast, energetic, young, hype — like a crypto YouTuber, NOT a movie narrator
crypto-promo.visuals: Screen recordings of OUR product zoomed/cropped, kinetic typography, motion graphics, NO static images
crypto-promo.text: Kinetic typography is the PRIMARY visual. Text animates in/out with SFX. Captions required for mute viewing
techniques: whoosh/impact SFX on every text hit
screen recording behind ALL text (no dead dark frames)
knownFailures: static text on flat dark bg = dead air
knownSuccesses: game home page hero art is beautiful
lessonsFromV1: Screen recordings must be QC'd frame-by-frame for loading states, errors, cursors, URL bars
Every text appearance needs a whoosh/impact SFX
Text-on-dark is dead air — always have screen recording or motion behind text
Music must be genre-appropriate (electronic/trap, NOT cinematic orchestral)
Music volume should be 0.4-0.5, not 0.25 — the beat drives energy
VO should be 1.2-1.3x speed for hype pacing
Zoom into specific UI elements instead of showing full-page scrolls
Captions/subtitles required — 85% of Twitter videos watched on mute
Show populated states (cards owned, progress made), not empty inventories
Include social proof and urgency (presale dates, holder counts)
Add Polygon/ERC-721/ERC-20 badges for crypto credibility
CTA must be specific action (Mint Now, Join Discord), not just URL
provenClipSources: Illuvium auto-battler gameplay (hex grid combat, creature abilities, board setup)
Abstract blockchain node network animation (dark bg, no branding)
Cosmic nebula space footage (purple/gold, matches D&D brand)
Neon tunnel/abstract tech animations (for AI/tech sections)
NFT card reveal animations (cropped to remove branding)

provenColors.white: general text, readable over any background

product-demo.description: Walkthrough of the actual game/product features for interested users
product-demo.music: Lo-fi or ambient electronic, low volume
product-demo.visuals: Clean screen recordings with zoom/highlight annotations
product-demo.text: Feature labels, callout arrows, step numbers

social-clip.description: 5-15 second micro-clips for engagement farming on Twitter/TikTok
social-clip.music: Trending audio or punchy beat drop
social-clip.visuals: Single wow moment — card reveal, 3D model, combat clip
